question.title: SimpleFlatMapper and jOOQ fails on JSON columns
question.body:
I am querying a table representing a file on a Posgress DB using the frameworks jOOQ and SFM. The table among others columns has and md5 checksum and a details columns whose type is JSONObject. I am using the POJOS generated by the jOOQ for setting the mapping.

I get an error when executing the following test case:

public void testSelectFile(){

    String md5 = "dfsg3456gfhg";

    JdbcMapper<File> jdbcMapper = JdbcMapperFactory.newInstance()
            .newMapper(File.class);

    Function<File, File> fileFileFunction = l -> l;
    try (DSLContext context = DSL.using(getDataSource(), SQLDialect.POSTGRES)) {
        try (ResultSet rs = context.selectFrom(FILE).where(FILE.MD5.eq(md5)).fetchResultSet()) {
             jdbcMapper.stream(rs)
                    .findAny()
                    .map(fileFileFunction::apply)
                    .orElseThrow(NotFoundException::new);
        } catch (SQLException ex) {
        }
    } catch (NotFoundException e) {
    }
}

The error obtained is this:

java.lang.NullPointerException
at org.simpleflatmapper.util.TypeHelper.toClass(TypeHelper.java:29)
at org.simpleflatmapper.util.TypeHelper.toBoxedClass(TypeHelper.java:171)
at org.simpleflatmapper.util.TypeHelper.isAssignable(TypeHelper.java:144)
at org.simpleflatmapper.converter.ConverterService.findConverter(ConverterService.java:62)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.meta.MapClassMeta.<init>(MapClassMeta.java:30)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.ReflectionService.newMapMeta(ReflectionService.java:163)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.ReflectionService.newClassMeta(ReflectionService.java:150)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.ReflectionService.getClassMeta(ReflectionService.java:128)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.meta.PropertyMeta.newPropertyClassMeta(PropertyMeta.java:53)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.meta.PropertyMeta.getPropertyClassMeta(PropertyMeta.java:46)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.meta.ObjectPropertyFinder.lookForSubProperty(ObjectPropertyFinder.java:113)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.meta.ObjectPropertyFinder.lookForConstructor(ObjectPropertyFinder.java:66)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.meta.ObjectPropertyFinder.lookForProperties(ObjectPropertyFinder.java:38)
at org.simpleflatmapper.reflect.meta.PropertyFinder.findProperty(PropertyFinder.java:28)
at org.simpleflatmapper.map.mapper.PropertyMappingsBuilder._addProperty(PropertyMappingsBuilder.java:95)
at org.simpleflatmapper.map.mapper.PropertyMappingsBuilder.addProperty(PropertyMappingsBuilder.java:73)
at org.simpleflatmapper.map.mapper.ConstantSourceMapperBuilder.addMapping(ConstantSourceMapperBuilder.java:109)
at org.simpleflatmapper.map.mapper.AbstractMapperBuilder.addMapping(AbstractMapperBuilder.java:171)
at org.simpleflatmapper.jdbc.JdbcMapperFactory$SetRowMapperFactory.newInstance(JdbcMapperFactory.java:310)
at org.simpleflatmapper.jdbc.JdbcMapperFactory$SetRowMapperFactory.newInstance(JdbcMapperFactory.java:298)
at org.simpleflatmapper.map.mapper.DynamicSetRowMapper.getMapper(DynamicSetRowMapper.java:101)
at org.simpleflatmapper.map.mapper.DynamicSetRowMapper.getMapperFromSet(DynamicSetRowMapper.java:91)
at org.simpleflatmapper.map.mapper.DynamicSetRowMapper.stream(DynamicSetRowMapper.java:74)
at org.simpleflatmapper.jdbc.JdbcMapperFactory$DynamicJdbcSetRowMapper.stream(JdbcMapperFactory.java:251)

The versions I am using are:

  • org.jooq:jooq:3.10.4
  • org.simpleflatmapper:sfm-jooq:3.13.4

I am wondering whether I am missing some configuration or whether I have to better define the mapper so not to fail on JSONObjet columns.

Any hint or comment is going to be much appreaciated.

EDIT 1 I've managed to work around the issue by choosing the columns I need to retrieve by not including the JSONObject one. For instance the following test case code work out:

public void testSelectFile(){

    String md5 = "dfsg3456gfhg";

    JdbcMapper<File> jdbcMapper = JdbcMapperFactory.newInstance()
        .newMapper(File.class);

    final List<Field<?>> columns = new ArrayList<Field<?>>();
    columns.add(FILE.ID);
    columns.add(FILE.FILENAME);

    Function<File, File> fileFileFunction = l -> l;
    try (DSLContext context = DSL.using(getDataSource(), SQLDialect.POSTGRES)) {
        try (ResultSet rs = context.select(columns).from(FILE).where(FILE.MD5.eq(md5)).fetchResultSet()) {
             jdbcMapper.stream(rs)
                .findAny()
                .map(fileFileFunction::apply)
                .orElseThrow(NotFoundException::new);
        } catch (SQLException ex) {
        }
    } catch (NotFoundException e) {
    }
}

It is worth to pint out that this is not going to fix the issue regarding JSONObject, it only provides a way to avoid the issue.
